_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Acute onset of severe abdominal pain or chest pain
Cyanosis, fever, vomiting, and bloody stool (less frequently)
Refusal to feed, vomiting, and decreased activity (typically 2-5 days post-onset)
Sudden onset of abdominal pain, distension, ileus, respiratory distress (most common presenting manifestation)
Free intraperitoneal air on radiological imaging (for diagnosis)

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication of procedure and planned line of management

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with treatment details
FIR/MLC in case of trauma cases (Optional)

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history especially medication history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, indication for procedure?
Did clinical presentation and imaging (Free air will be visualized on plain x-rays or the CT scan) confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the imaging indicative of surgery?
Was FIR/MLC filed in case of trauma (optional)?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical presentation and imaging were not indicative of surgery for perforation.
Clinical notes are incomplete or missing details regarding history (including medication history), signs, symptoms, planned treatment, or indication for the procedure.
Clinical presentation and imaging (e.g., plain x-rays, CT scan showing free air) did not confirm the diagnosis of perforation.
Relevant imaging (X-ray Chest/Abdomen / USG/CT Abdomen) is missing or insufficient for pre-authorization.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) with treatment details are missing or incomplete at claim submission.
Detailed procedure or operative notes are missing at claim submission.
Detailed discharge summary is missing or incomplete at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Perforation of the stomach is a full-thickness injury of the wall of the organ.

Etiology: Etiology is unknown in many young people. Gastric injury can be the consequence of: Peptic ulcer disease, Spontaneous Gastric Perforation, Trauma, Malignancy-Related Gastric Perforation, Iatrogenic.
additional_information.pre_op_preparation: Initial management: Intravenous antibiotics, Correct fluid and electrolyte imbalance.
